### Ojo Caliente/Tres Piedras Vicinity

• 
$50,000 | 10 acres | Lot 8 Cerrito De Baca Rd, Ojo Caliente | Water: Unknown | Road: County | Source: Multiple (Sotheby's Int. RE/Washington)

• 
$50,000 | 10 acres | Lot 8 Cerrito De Baca Rd, Ojo Caliente | Water: Unknown | Road: County | Source: Trulia

### Market Notes

Taos County ranks 20th among 33 counties for combined acreage of rural properties. Over $359 million in land listings account for 9,555 acres, with average price of $560,282
. 
Approximately 6,000 acres of undeveloped land listed countywide with average price of $230,721
.

**Off-grid availability:** 
Tres Piedras Estates zoned County Rural Area, allowing RV living for 180 days without building permit; off-grid dwellings (earthship homes, tiny homes, shipping containers) permitted
. 
Carson Estates similarly permits RV placement for 180 days and alternative housing types
.

**Subdivision structure limitation:** 
Tres Piedras Estates primarily subdivided into quarter-acre parcels; parcels larger than quarter-acre require purchase of adjacent lots
.

## REAL ESTATE AGENTS & BROKERS—TAOS COUNTY VACANT LAND SPECIALISTS

### Hemingway Land Company
• **Specialization:** 
Raw/vacant undeveloped land in Arizona, Colorado, Hawaii, Nevada, New Mexico

• **Service Area:** 
Tres Piedras Estates, Carson Estates, Highland Estates subdivisions

• **Key Features:** 
Affordable New Mexico land; easy owner financing available on every listing

• **Contact:** Hemingway Land Company, Las Vegas, Nevada | hemingwayland.com
• **Note:** Specializes in owner-financed off-grid properties; manages hundreds of quarter-acre to multi-acre parcels across subdivisions

### Berkshire Hathaway HomeServices Taos Real Estate
• **Specialization:** 
Number 1 brokerage in Taos Valley since 1987; extensive luxury properties, condominiums, mountain homes, Taos Ski Valley real estate, and ranches

• **Land Coverage:** Active in
sections.builders:
## ZOOK CABINS (IRC Modular Assembled Delivery)

**Pricing & 2025-2026 Changes**
- 
Base starting price for Rockwood model reported at $132,500

- 
Delivery costs included in advertised price (distinguishing factor from competitors)

- Specific pricing for Heritage, Grove ADU, and Rivara ADU models not publicly listed; 
pricing with delivery to specific zip codes available on website

- 
Company on Inc. 5000 list for 2024 and 2025; featured on Financial Times' "Americas' Fastest-Growing Companies" list for 2025

**Customer Reviews**
- Positive: 
Customer testimonials predominantly positive, with clients frequently praising professionalism, helpfulness, high workmanship quality, seamless process, and beauty of final product
. 
Customers expressed delight with quality, noting even contractors were impressed

- Negative: 
Some prospective customers found pricing higher than anticipated

- 
Conflicting information exists regarding Better Business Bureau accreditation status

**New Mexico Delivery & Availability**
- 
Modular homes typically built and delivered within 12 months, though timing varies based on project queue

- 
Family-owned business with 15+ years building modular log cabins; intent on making New Mexico experience smooth

- 
Documented delivery to Chama, New Mexico

**Model Status**
- Heritage: Status unclear from search results
- 
Grove ADU: A-frame design with 2 modules (525 sq ft + 270 sq ft), certified modular, offers 1 or 2 bedroom floor plans

- 
Rivara ADU: Modern, clean classic design; certified modular; perfectly suited for ADU use or primary residence

**Notable Features**
- 
Delivered fully assembled; rollback-style trailers navigate tight spaces; some models require crane delivery

## MIGHTY SMALL HOMES (SIP Panel Kit)

**Pricing & 2025-2026 Changes**
- 
Cost estimate $140–$328+ per square foot, all-inclusive (kit, labor, site work)

- Price information unchanged since December 2021; no recent 2025-2026 updates found
- 
Freight: approximately $4 per mile, minimum $1,200 charge from Louisville, Kentucky

**Customer Reviews**
- Limited public reviews located; company focuses on technical specifications over testimonials
- No negative reviews or complaint patterns documented in search results

**New Mexico Delivery & Availability**
- Delivery feasible to New Mexico (operates nationwide)
- 
Freight to New Mexico (~900 miles from Louisville) would total approximately $3,600–$4,000 before minimum charge applies

**Model Status**
- 
A-Frame 506sf: Eliminates interior load
sections.offgrid:
**NM Solar Incentives 2025–2026**

A bill in the 2026 NM Legislature proposes increasing the state solar tax credit from 10% to 30% with the cap increasing from $6,000 to $15,000.

Federal residential solar tax credit expired January 1, 2026, following legislation signed by President Trump on July 4, 2025.
 https://350newmexico.org/solar-and-battery-storage/

**Taos County Land Use Regulations Updates**

Taos County has hired Architectural Research Consultants, Inc. to revise and update the Land Use Regulations (Ordinance 2018-2) and Subdivision Regulations (Ordinance 2005-8), modernizing codes to meet current conditions while encouraging sustainable development practices.
 https://www.taoscounty.org/492/Land-Use-and-Subdivision-Regulations-Upd

**Earthship Communities — Northern New Mexico**

There are around 200 Earthships in Northern New Mexico.

The Greater World Earthship Community is the world's largest off-grid, legal subdivision, sitting on 630 acres just 15 minutes northwest of Taos.

A YouTube documentary featuring founder Michael Reynolds was released November 15, 2025.
 https://www.greaterworldearthship.com

**Off-Grid Living in Taos Region**

Hundreds of people live off-the-grid on Taos Mesa outside Taos.

Off-grid community residents use natural building materials including straw bale, earth bags, cob, rammed earth, and adobe.
 https://taosmesa.us/

**Water Rights & Well Drilling — Northern Areas**

Northern and cold areas including Taos have ground freeze depth of 1–3 feet; drilling rigs must be winterized.

Hard rock (basalt, granite, limestone) in northern and mountainous regions requires specialized equipment; deeper wells over 500 ft in mountainous areas can cost $30,000–$70,000 or more.
 https://www.ose.nm.gov/Statewide/

$1,200 discount on any new 16'–30' yurt ordered by April 16, 2026, plus bonus offer
.

.

## RV / 5TH WHEEL UNDER $45K

Search results show dealer listings and price ranges but lack specific inventory with exact prices near Taos/7,000-foot elevation. 

Fifth wheel price range is $15,000 to $100,000
.

**Dealers in Region:**
- Myers RV Center (Albuquerque, NM): https://www.myersrv.com/fifth-wheels
- Ram Motors RV & Truck Center (Albuquerque, NM): https://www.rammotors.net
- Windisch RV Center (Denver/Colorado Springs, CO): https://www.windishrv.com/
- RV Trader classifieds: 
240 listings for used fifth wheels in Colorado
; 
86 used fifth wheels in New Mexico

Individual listings require direct dealer contact; pricing subject to rapid change.
